contextBanner: [TBG | Unified Launch Surfaces | observer-first path invariance | identity-frozen actions | Steam visibility]
purpose: Use one observer-first run context for ForgeContinue, Auto Launch Nav, new-game Play, Steam-mediated launch, and future registered launch paths; observe every correlated launcher, calibration, modal, Steam-broker, and game-handoff window; freeze identity per surface action; reconcile with bounded polling; and emit normalized runtime-observer envelopes without weakening UI Automation, quarantine, multitasking, or proof safeguards.
currentBestStrategy: every launch path uses the same observer-first run context and event cascade before actuation
launcher-window-context.json owns PLAY versus CONTINUE and freezes the target PID and HWND
each later calibration, Safe Mode, Caution, other launcher, Steam-broker, or handoff surface receives an independently frozen identity inside the same run
the Windows listener uses SetWinEventHook for create, show, hide, destroy, title, and focus changes, while polling remains reconciliation rather than the primary signal
the launch reconciliation poll remains 100 milliseconds and slows only after a stable bounded interval
known CAUTION and Safe Mode windows are identified from direct metadata and acted on through exact named controls
calibration is observed and quarantined from action until an exact semantic action and fixture are registered
Steam and steamwebhelper windows are observed only when correlated to the active launch and never receive automatic action authority
window-intelligence observations are adapted into TbgWindowLifecycleRuntimeEvent.v1 and reduced by the pure lifecycle reducer
module XML comparison predicts dependency cautions before they appear but prediction alone never authorizes a click
unknown windows enter unknown_quarantined and use the existing S1/S2 snapshot delta once to produce a learning candidate
images and OCR remain diagnostic fallback only
allowedScope: read UI Automation names, automation identifiers, control types, enabled state, and bounding metadata
read module dependency declarations and installed module versions
read the registered launch path and shared run context
register and remove read-only Windows SetWinEventHook callbacks
observe Steam and steamwebhelper windows only when correlated to the active launch
create and pass lifecycle run and correlation authority into the metadata watcher
append canonical runtime lifecycle events and materialize ignored run artifacts
reduce supplied events into deterministic lifecycle state and transition objects without side effects
write ignored cache, result, report, event, progress, handoff, lease, and learning-candidate artifacts
invoke an exact registered named modal control when all direct action signals are present
write window observations into the universal state journal
forbiddenScope: guess PLAY versus CONTINUE outside launcher-window-context.json
bypass the shared observer contract from ForgeContinue, Auto Launch Nav, new-game, Steam-mediated, or future paths
act before the required observers and run context are active
automatically click an unknown, calibration, or Steam window
globally enumerate unrelated Steam windows as Bannerlord launch surfaces
use prediction alone as click authority
learn coordinate positions as semantic identity
silently retarget a frozen surface action after a broad scan
promote a spawned Singleplayer host back into launcher-menu selection
treat screenshot parsing as the primary strategy
perform live clicking, process launch, focus mutation, sleeps, or runtime file writes inside the lifecycle reducer or runtime adapter
click, foreground, send keys to, or automatically act on an observed window from the event listener
promote action dispatch or window disappearance into application acceptance
claim campaign readiness, command acknowledgement, movement, arrival, trade, or live product success from window recognition or lifecycle reduction
edit skills, capabilities, operations, or artifact-engine trigger routing outside the registered launcher-lifecycle ownership

requires: the unified launch-surface doctrine fixture and validator pass
every registered launch path declares the same run-context, observer, artifact, event, and proof contract
Play/Continue, calibration, Safe Mode, Caution, Steam broker, other launcher windows, and Singleplayer handoff are represented in the path-surface matrix
the registry and policy parse
the lifecycle state, transition, run-context, and runtime-event schemas parse
the lifecycle reducer contains no launch, click, focus, sleep, coordinate, image, or OCR primitive
the runtime adapter imports the pure reducer and materializes the registered artifact set
known modal fixture events reduce from unseen through action_ready and action_dispatched
window disappearance preserves action_dispatch as the proof ceiling
the Singleplayer host reduces to terminal_observation only after canonical identity resolution
an unknown window enters unknown_quarantined and rejects action authority
calibration remains observed but action-blocked until an exact action contract and fixture exist
a correlated Steam broker window is recorded and never receives action authority
unrelated Steam windows remain outside the correlated launch scope
duplicate and out-of-order runtime sequences are rejected without mutating accepted state
an illegal dispatch from unseen or recognized-without-authority is rejected without state mutation
fixture simulation never emits action_dispatched as a live mutation claim
the screenshot-derived fixture resolves to bannerlord.dependency-version-caution
all four dependencies and both versions are parsed
Confirm is the only automatic action for the dependency caution
Safe Mode maps to No and never Cancel
a predicted mismatch without direct modal signals cannot dispatch
an unknown window produces a learning candidate and no action
the launcher context starts the metadata watcher with lifecycle run and correlation authority
the listener hooks create/show/hide/destroy/title/focus events and keeps callback work to queueing metadata
the listener emits only normalized runtime-observer envelopes into its run root
the listener uses a 100 millisecond launch reconciliation poll and a slower stable poll
hook and poll duplicates are suppressed without collapsing legitimate repeat events
callback failure, listener restart, out-of-order input, and missed-event reconciliation fixtures pass
window disappearance remains an observation and never becomes application acceptance, process loss, or crash confirmation
every action claim has a fresh verified transition or an explicit unverified result
the result and English report preserve the proof boundary
PowerShell Core and Windows PowerShell validators pass
